task: I need to text a coworker about covering the last hour of my shift because something came up. What’s a simple way to word it so the handoff is clear and nothing gets missed?

turns.agent_response:
You could text:

**“Hey, something came up — can you cover my section for the last hour? Just wanted to make sure the handoff is clear and nothing gets missed. Appreciate it.”**

If you want, I can make it even shorter and more casual.

user_simulator.text:
Close enough, but make it a little more direct. I’d send:

“Hey, something came up and I need you to cover my last hour. My section should be fine, but I want the handoff clean so nothing gets missed. I’ve got [tables/notes] ready.”

If you want it to sound less stiff, swap that last line for:

“Everything’s set up, I just need you to take over the last hour.”

That’s cleaner. Less chatter.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — a clean, direct version would be:

**“Hey, something came up and I need you to cover my last hour. Everything’s set up, I just need you to take over so nothing gets missed.”**

If you want, I can make it even shorter or more casual.

user_simulator.text: Yeah. That works. Short, clear, no fluff.
